alternative_parents.description: Inorganic chemical compounds with one or more oxygen atoms combined with another element.
alternative_parents.name: Inorganic oxides

alternative_parents.description: Inorganic compounds of nitrogen where nitrogen has a formal oxidation state of -3.
alternative_parents.name: Inorganic nitrides

alternative_parents.description: Inorganic salts containing a lithium ion.
alternative_parents.name: Inorganic lithium salts

class.description: Inorganic salt compounds in which the heaviest ionic metal atom is an alkali metal.
class.name: Alkali metal salts

description: This compound belongs to the class of inorganic compounds known as alkali metal fluorides. These are inorganic compounds in which the largest halogen atom is fluorine, and the heaviest metal atom is an alkali metal.

direct_parent.description: Inorganic compounds in which the largest halogen atom is fluorine, and the heaviest metal atom is an alkali metal.
direct_parent.name: Alkali metal fluorides

kingdom.description: Compounds that do not contain a C atom. Exceptions are made for isocyanide/cyanide and their non-hydrocarbyl derivatives, thiophosgene, carbon diselenide, carbon monosulfide, carbon disulfide, carbon subsulfide, carbon monoxide, carbon trioxide, carbon suboxide, and dicarbon monoxide.
kingdom.name: Inorganic compounds

subclass.description: Inorganic compounds in which the largest halogen atom is fluorine, and the heaviest metal atom is an alkali metal.
subclass.name: Alkali metal fluorides

superclass.description: Compounds that contains both metal and non metal atoms.
superclass.name: Mixed metal/non-metal compounds
